subject: Biology
title: Classifying Microorganisms
description: Learn Classifying Microorganisms in one minute.

hook.text: Microbes are wildly diverse. Sorting them into groups took centuries of debate.

buildup.text: The three domains of life are Bacteria, Archaea, and Eukarya. Two of those are entirely microbial.

discovery.text: Carl Woese used ribosomal RNA to reclassify life, revealing Archaea as a separate domain in 1977.

twist.text: Archaea look like bacteria under a microscope but are genetically closer to us.

climax.text: Modern genomics keeps reshuffling the microbial family tree as new species are discovered.

punchline.text: The tree of life keeps growing new branches.

quiz.question: Who used ribosomal RNA to discover the domain Archaea?
